steps.title: Find the initial constant C
steps.content:
At the initial measurement, $t = 0$ and $P = 9000$. Substituting into $P = C(2)^{rt}$:
$$9000 = C(2)^{r \cdot 0} = C(2)^0$$
Since $(2)^0 = 1$:
$$9000 = C \cdot 1 = C$$
Therefore $C = 9000$.

steps.title: Substitute the 10-hour measurement and solve for r
steps.content:
At $t = 10$ hours, $P = 18000$. Substitute into the formula:
$$18000 = 9000(2)^{10r}$$
Divide both sides by 9000:
$$2 = (2)^{10r}$$
Rewrite $2$ as $2^1$:
$$2^1 = (2)^{10r}$$
Since the bases are equal, equate the exponents:
$$1 = 10r$$
$$r = \frac{1}{10}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
